observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere on proper recognition of an item of Property, Plant and Equipment as well as the fair presentation of financial statements provided in Philippine Application Guidance No. 3 of Paragraphs 19-20 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 1 and Paragraph 13 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) 17.  Likewise, instruct the Municipal Accountant to take up in books of accounts the unrecorded lots which are duly validated and evaluated by the Inventory Committee at their fair market value should the acquisition cost is not available.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office observe the proper accounts to record the financial transactions in the books of accounts and immediately make necessary adjustments to correct the identified erroneous accounting entries.
We recommended and Management agreed to undertake all the procedures under COA Circular 2020-006 to come up with the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office strictly observe compliance with the perpetual inventory method of accounting on the supplies and materials procured for construction projects implementation by administration by recording all purchases under the account Construction Materials Inventory. Also, coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer or designate a Municipal GSO who will be tasked to implement controls on the receipt and utilization of the same and submit a summary of the utilized construction materials monthly to the Accounting Office. Likewise, effect the necessary adjustment for the number of supplies and materials which remained unutilized for the intended projects or repairs.

We recommended and the Management agreed to enforce the deduction for retention or warranty security to adhere to the provisions prescribed in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184, not only in infrastructure projects but also in the procurement of goods. Henceforth, ensure that future procurements be deducted with at least 1% but not more than 5% retention money or require a special bank guarantee of the same amount.

We recommended that the Management refrain from entrusting the procurement process to the requesting office for goods not subject to public bidding.  Instead, delegate to the BAC, all procurement activities, may it be public bidding or alternative mode of procurement, to ensure the proper way of procuring based on RA 9184.
We recommended that Management instruct the heads of recipient offices and the BAC to give extra effort to implement projects funded by National Government Agencies.
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to communicate with the Source Agencies on what to do with the unutilized/excess funds.

We recommended that the Management stops immediately the remittance of GS Pag-IBIG contributions to HDMF in excess of the prescribed rate; request HDMF to consider the excess amount as advance payment of GS in future remittance, otherwise this may be refunded by officials and employees concerned; and strengthen the internal control system particularly the review of remittances on statutory obligations to prevent from recurrence of the deficiency.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure on-time remittance of barangays’ shares from RPT collections in the succeeding quarters as provided under Section 271 (d) of the Local Government Code.
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to correct the entry made.
observations.managementAction: Adjusted Already

observations.recommendation: a)	Prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JV) to adjust the properties below the capitalization threshold and its related accounts.    b)	Ensure that all future acquisitions of properties below the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000 will be recorded in accordance with Section 5.4 of COA Circular 2016-006 and Chapter II Section 4.ee of GAM for LGU Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Adjusted Already

observations.recommendation: Thus, we recommended that Management abides by the standards set forth by procurement law and its recent issuances such as COA-GPPB JMC No. 1 and GPPB Circular 01-2020 for COVID-19 emergency procurements.
observations.managementAction: We will abide in future procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor and follow-up the submission of reports and other supporting documents every end of the month to avoid the same condition.
observations.managementAction: With the new Treasurer this condition will improve, as we will work out closely hand in hand.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to secure documentary requirements before payment, especially under Emergency Purchase, so as not to compromise integrity and transparency in public procurement that could be disadvantageous to the government.
observations.managementAction: We are still pursuing submission of the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to submit the lacking documents and ensure the complete and propriety of the required documents for all contracts submitted to COA for review under COA Circular No. 2009 001 to avoid suspensions in the audit.
observations.managementAction: Lacking documents already submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that completed projects are timely transferred to its appropriate asset account to commence its depreciation in order to provide correct and proper information on the face of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Implemented the COA’s propose adjusting entry.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the collecting officer to submit justification on the discrepancies noted on the issued Official Receipt No. 8446263. Likewise, direct her to strictly supervise the Collecting Officers in the proper filling up of the necessary details in the Official Receipt.
observations.managementAction: Submit justification (a) Noted discrepancy could not be ascertained since the concerned collecting officer no longer connected with the Municipal Tree Park or with the LGU                    (b) Strict supervision of the collecting officer especially in the proper filling up of the necessary details in the Official Receipt

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agree to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the documents containing adequate information as basis for the recording of the Other Investment Account.
observations.managementAction: Tracing of supporting documents is remote. The transaction occurred more than 12 years ago. Municipal Accountant and other Accounting staff not yet connected with the LGU during the occurrence of the transaction.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer ensure accurate assessment of business taxes by following the proper procedures as specified in the Local Treasury Operations Manual (LTOM) and by checking of books of accounts and/or ledger of business establishment with corresponding Sworn Statement Gross Receipts/Sales and Income Tax Return in accordance to Items (d) & (e) of Section 2C.04 of the Municipal Revised Revenue Code of 2016. Moreover, correct the erroneous interpretation in the computation of taxes based on the tax table.
observations.managementAction: To follow the proper procedures as specified in the LTOM.                      To verify or assess the Sworn Statement of Gross Sales and Receipts of Taxpayer before issuance of business permit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality re-evaluate its capacity to undertake the projects by administration, taking into account, the bulk of projects, the availability of man power, tools and equipment. Furthermore, take necessary and appropriate actions to achieve full implementation of the projects so that the desired services and benefits be delivered to its beneficiaries promptly.
observations.managementAction: To implement some of the projects by contract.

observations.recommendation: We recommend and the Management agreed to direct the BAC to follow the correct format of Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and Procurement Monitoring Report (PAR) as prescribed by the revised IRR of RA 9184, and include in the APP the procurement of infrastructure projects. Moreover, ensure that submitted contracts and its supporting documents for contract review are complete and comply with all the standard set of forth in the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: To follow COA recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend and the management assured to properly account the materials and labor that were incurred in the partial implementation of the project. Moreover, fast track the implementation of LGSF - SALINTUBIG to ensure that the desired benefits could be delivered to the constituents on time, and strictly follow the provisions stated in DILG Memorandum Circular 2018-61) Policy Guidelines and Procedures in the Implementation of FY 2018 Local Government Support Fund) to be directed of proper actions to be done with regard to the implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: To fast track the implementation of the project LGSF - SALINTUBIG.

observations.recommendation: We recommend and the management agreed to direct Municipal Accountant to prepare a journal entry reclassifying the outstanding receivables granted to individual beneficiaries recorded in the Due from NGO/POs account to the Other Receivables account. Moreover, send demand letters to the debtors and enforce collection thereof.
observations.managementAction: (a) To reclassify the outstanding receivables from Due from NGO/POs account to Other Receivables Account.       (b)To reinforce collection by sending demand letter to the debtors.

observations.recommendation:
(a) Conduct physical inventory on all PPE owned by the LGU and submit the Report thereon to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. 
  (b) Perform reconciliation of property cards to ensure that all discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled with the books of accounts;
observations.managementAction:
Conducted Physical Inventory on all PPE

Reconciliation is still ongoing to be worked on by the Municipal Treasurer at year end 12/2020

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer coordinates with the Municipal Assessor to produce the financial records needed that will constitute the correct basis for the recording of the RPT/SET Receivables. Moreover, instruct her staff to regularly update the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index cards to facilitate the accurate presentation of the RPT/SET Receivables in the books.
observations.managementAction: For implementation by the new treasurer in 2020-2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management exert effort in collecting past due accounts to ensure collectability and use the funds earned on its operation under the economic enterprise.

We recommended Management for the full implementation of the projects so that the desired services and benefits be delivered to its beneficiaries promptly. Also, employ regular monitoring mechanisms in assessing the rate of progress of project implementation in order to achieve the desired level of efficiency.
observations.managementAction: New Management Committee to fast tract implementation this year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management for the full implementation of the projects so that the desired services and benefits be delivered to its constituents promptly. Also, employ regular monitoring mechanisms in assessing the rate of progress of project implementation in order to achieve the desired level of efficiency.
observations.managementAction: New Management Committee to fast tract implementation this year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the intra-agency receivables and intra-payables accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances thus ensuring the presentation of reliable date on the financial reports of the LGU as of a given period of time.
observations.managementAction: Already reconciled at Year End of 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit immediately to COA the original contracts and all pertinent supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: New management sees to it that contracts are available before start of the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create the mandatory positions for LDRRMO and appoint personnel who met the qualification standards set in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 in order to institutionalize the LDRRMO’s organizational structure and manpower compliment aimed to carry out the DRRM plans and programs in the long run.

We recommended that Management prepare the 5-Year Staffing Plan and Staff Transition Strategy and Business Plan to comply with the legal provisions and other requirements governing LEEs pursuant to Item 5.2, 5.3 and 6.1 of LBC 111.
observations.managementAction: To be worked out by new management in 2021 with appropriation of  P200,000.00 in 2021 for the plan

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the GFPS to administer HGDG for those LGU programs and projects attributed wholly or a portion thereof during the preparation of the GAD plan and budget and the accomplishment report.

We recommended that management issue demand letters to all officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances. If they refuse to heed the demand, instruct the Municipal Accountant to deduct from the salary to be applied to their outstanding cash advances in accordance with the above-cited COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: Balances of unliquidated cash advances were deducted from their salary.

observations.recommendation: We    recommended    that   management   direct   the     Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the provisions of Section 7.2.1a of the COA Circular No. 2009-006 by submitting disbursement vouchers and financial reports within the time frame.
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to correct the entry made.

We recommended to Management to direct the LDRRM Officer to prepare the LDRRMF Investment Plan every year and update the same if there are reprogrammed of the PPAs.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Budget Officer, as the one who is certifying the existence of available appropriation, to ensure that the use of savings under Section 321 of RA No. 7160 is covered by an Appropriation Ordinance providing for a supplemental budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management request the Municipal Sagguniang Bayan to issue a resolution authorizing the closure of the bank accounts maintained at the Philippine National Bank pursuant to Section 311 of RA 7160 to immediately transfer the total amount of ₱10,493,766.84 to either Land Bank of the Philippines or Development 	Bank of the Philippines to protect government funds.

We also recommended management to direct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the said amount to authorized government depository bank.
observations.managementAction: 5% of the total amount was left subject to further reconciliation between the LGUs books and the PNB. Total closure is on process by the MTO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should stop immediately the 	practice of transferring funds to Enterprise Bank. Instead the payment of 	salaries and wages of municipal employees should be drawn by the 	Municipal Treasurer through a cash advance or via an Automated Teller 	Machine (ATM) from either Land Bank of the Philippines or Development Bank of the Philippines to protect government funds.
observations.managementAction: Payment of salaries and wages through Enterprise Bank was already stopped.
observations.reason: Management stopped the practice of releasing their salaries and wages through Enterprise Bank. Salaries and wages of the employees of the LGU are made through the Municipal Treasurer’s cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management compel its Local Solid Waste Management Board to prepare a 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan 	to comply with the requirement of RA 9003 as basis of comprehensive 	action to address the Solid Waste management problem of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The 10-year SWM Plan was already approved by the Sangguniang Bayan and submitted to EMB but returned for some revisions. Revision is awaiting approval by the SB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop immediately the practice 	of spending government funds for religious or private purposes being  	violative of Section 335 of Republic Act No. 7160.

observations.reason: The management already implemented the audit recommendation of the team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to direct the following personnel/offices to:

Inventory Committee:

1.Conduct the physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment.

2.Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE by type showing the balance of property and equipment per cards and per count and the shortage/overage, if any.

3.Prepare the report yearly in three copies, certified correct by the Inventory Committee and approved by the Head of the Agency, and submit to the Office of the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year.  

Accounting Office:

1.Reconcile the report of the inventory committee with the control accounts in the general ledger.

2.Determine the discrepancy and effect adjustments in the books, if warranted.
3.	Maintain and update regularly the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC).

Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer:
 
1.Prepare the inventory guidelines, indicating the date of physical inventory, who will supervise the count, the personnel to act as counters and checkers, and the sequence of the counting, for approval by the Municipal Mayor.

2.Maintain and update regularly the Property/Equipment Cards for each class of fixed asset.
observations.managementAction:
One Job Order staff is assigned in Accounting Office:

To reconcile data between Treasurer and Accounting Records segregating serviceable, non-serviceable existing and non-existing.

-prepare General Masterlist and by Office of PPE.
-conduct physical inventory and post sticker on each property.

-report of PPE physical inventory for finalization.
observations.reason:
Submitted the Inventory of PPE but did not maintain property, plant and equipment ledger cards and property cards wherein all information pertaining to the LGU’s property, plant and equipment are recorded. 
And the inventory of PPE was  also  performed by one person only from the Accounting office

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold taxes on the honoraria or incentive pay given to national government employees stationed in the municipality in accordance with Section 2.79 of Revenue Regulation (RR) N0. 2-98 as amended by Section 3 of RR No.10-2008.
observations.managementAction: The honorarium or incentive pay given to national government employees        were already subjected to withholding tax
observations.reason: National Government Employess are subjected to withholding tax

observations.reason: Balance of retention was not released until compliance of the required documents. And the agency waited the final inspection from the COA before they released the retention money.

observations.recommendation:

1.	Strictly follow the gulidelines set under COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the entitlement, release, use, reporting and audit of confidential and/or intelligence funds.

2.	Submit justification why the above violation was committed together with the complete supporting documents of all disbursements listed above for further evaluation and to avoid audit suspension of the above transactions.
observations.managementAction: Succeeding liquidation of confidential expenses was in accordance with the guidelines set .
observations.reason: The management is compliant with the guidelines.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management direct the personnel concerned to submit the corresponding contracts of the above procurement and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) days upon receipt of this Audit Observation Memorandum in order to avoid audit suspensions on the above-stated transactions.  We also recommend management to strictly observe Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 to allow the audit team to conduct a timely review of the procurement involved.

observations.reason: Contracts and supporting documents were  received  by COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that in the preparation of the SEF budget, the members of the Local School Board give priority to programs, activities and projects enumerated in Section 272 of RA 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circulars Nos. 01, 01-A and 01-B and refrain from charging expenditures which are not prescribed by law for which SEF can be disposed of.
observations.managementAction: SEF budget was revised by the Local School Board giving priority to programs, activities, and projects in accordance with SEC. 272 of RA 7160 and DECS, DBm and DILG
observations.reason: SEF expenditures  were in accordance with the guidelines.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

1.	Require all implementing offices to submit Quarterly Reports on Government Projects, Programs and Activities from all sources to the Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator who will then consolidate the reports for submission to municipal officials for monitoring and decision making purposes.

2.	Conduct regular periodic meetings with the heads of departments/offices to assess whether the implementation of PPAs is proceeding as planned and to ensure that implementation constraints, if any, are immediately resolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to stop the practice of using Special Education Fund (SEF) for purposes other than those allowed under DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 and 01-A. The municipal officials should strictly observe the guidelines set to ensure optimal utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: Management discontinued already the practice of using SEF and strictly observing the guidelines set
observations.reason: The management stop the practice of using SEF for office supplies and equipment for Treasurer’s Office and Accounting Office usage.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality refrain from disbursing funds not appropriated for food and/ or representation expenses but rather provide for representation expenses in the future budgets.  In addition, refrain from or exercise prudence in incurring expenses for meals and snacks served during regular meetings, sessions or hearings to be responsive to the needs of the constituents and to ensure that government resources are used equitably and wisely.
observations.managementAction: Representation expenses were already provided in the Budget of CY 2015 and CY 2016.
observations.reason: Representation expenses was provided in the Annual Budget

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the following personnel/Offices:

Inventory Committee:
1.	Conduct the physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment.
2.	Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE by type showing the balance of property and equipment per cards and per count and the shortage/overage, if any.
3.	Prepare the report yearly in three copies, certified correct by the Inventory Committee and approved by the Head of the Agency, and submit to the Office of the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year.  For CY 2014, submit the report on or before March 31, 2015.

Accounting Office:
1.	Reconcile the report of the inventory committee with the control accounts in the general ledger.
2.	Determine the discrepancy and effect adjustments in the books, if warranted.
3.	Maintain and update regularly the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC)

Municipal Treasurer/ Property Officer:
1.	Prepare the inventory guidelines, indicating the date of physical inventory, who is to supervise the count, the personnel to act as counters and checkers, and the sequence of the counting, for approval by the Municipal Mayor.
2.	Maintain and update regularly the Property/Equipment Cards for each class of fixed asset.

- reconcile data between Treasurer and Accounting segregating serviceable, non serviceable existing and non existing
-prepare general masterlist and by office PPE

-report of PPE physical inventory for finalization

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records as of March 30, 2015 of all existing bank accounts and to furnish this office the reconciliation statements of the three (3) records, namely, cash book maintained by the treasurer, subsidiary ledger of the accountant and bank statements, on or before April 30, 2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to coordinate and exert effort to submit immediately the accounts for the months of September to December of 2014 and succeeding reports should be submitted within the timeframe set under the said regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the in-charge in the preparation of purchase orders to completely indicate all the necessary information.  In addition, direct the municipal accountant to strictly impose the policy that procured items with incompletely filled up POs/contracts and other supporting documents shall not be processed for payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop the practice of disbursing funds without proper documentations and approval and to strictly adhere to the guidelines set under DBM National Budget Circular No. 446 and 446-A.

The management stop the practice of disbursing funds without proper documentations.

They also avoid hiring van rental.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the management:

1.	Submit a written explanation/justification on the importance of the attendance of the officials to the said Lakbay-Aral and evidence of invitation or approval from the host LGU and DILG.
2.	Submit evidence that LGU of Marihatag was a contingent/participant during the Sinulog 2014 Festival, such as, but not limited to, program of activity showing Municipality of Marihatag as contingent and the City of Cebu’s certification on the participation of LGU-Marihatag.
3.	Observe the austerity measures set by proper authority, minimize attendance to Lakbay Aral instead, appropriate Agency funds for projects/activities that brings immediate socio-economic benefits among local constituents.
